title: Crowds continued to pour into the CJP's protest site at Jantar Mantar through the day as preparations gathered pace for Monday's march to Parliament
author: Shrimansi  Kaushik,Nikhil M Babu
content_html:
On Sunday (July 19, 2026) afternoon, thousands of people were flowing through both entries to the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protest site at Jantar Mantar in central Delhi. With thousands of people inside the protest area, it was almost impossible to walk without bumping into another person. 

The crowd has seen a dramatic increase compared to the initial days of the protest, which started on June 20.

People who have been at the site for many days said that there has been a marked increase in the crowd after police forcefully removed activist Sonam Wangchuk from the protest site on Saturday (July 18, 2026) morning. 

At any given time, there was a queue of people trying to enter the protest site at both entries. Once you enter, at multiple points, protestors doubled up as crowd managers, while some volunteers fanned for other people and other volunteers sprayed water from handheld sprays as people moved through queues. 

Standing next to a table filled with medicines and masks, Abhishek Saha, an Ayurvedic doctor from Rohini in north Delhi, was busy giving ORS packets, tablets, and band-aids to people coming in with various issues at regular intervals.

“It was started by another doctor, who was from Maharashtra, and now I’m looking after it. People have donated all the medicines,” Mr. Saha said, adding that he was expressing his solidarity for a better education system. 

The table acts as a makeshift clinic for the people visiting the protest and many living there for many days now. 

There was heavy deployment of Rapido Action Force, Nagaland Armed Force and Delhi Police personnel. At least 10-11 buses were seen near both entry points. Crossing multiple layers of barricades protestors found themselves in a sea of people, with AISA, SFI, and other student organisation camps where students on hunger strike rested amid heat and humidity. Volunteers of Krantikari Yuva Sangathan (KYS) fanned the protesters who were seated, listening to the leaders on the dias. Several groups sang songs and raised placards demanding resignation of the Education Minister. 

Pawan Kumar, 28, from Uttar Pradesh, and Shambu Dayal Meena, 31, from Rajasthan, said that they met at the protest site and have been fasting for the past six days. 

“There is a lot of anger against the current government. They don’t respect the Constitution,” Mr. Meena, a tribal, said. “When the CJI said cockroaches, it felt bad because it felt like he was talking about me. I had been unemployed in the past,” he said. 

Sitting next to them, Janeshwar, 32, said, referring to the trio, “Here, a Dalit, a tribal, and an OBC person are sitting together and protesting. Why? Because the government has closed down thousands of schools and it is our children that suffer. Not the children of the rich.”

Ravi, a student from Rai Bareilly, Uttar Pradesh, said his brother had appeared for NEET this year. “Students work hard and give their everything to government exams only to realise they have been leaked. I cannot even imagine the state of parents who lost their children to these scams,” he said. 

“ I am glad that so many people have turned up today,” said 60-year-old Vinod Kumar Mehra, a social worker from Gurgaon. “For the youth who want to change the country, hunger strike is the only option to express their rage and that is what they are doing,” he said. 

People turned up not just to demand the resignation of the Union Education Minister. “It is a platform for us to express our discontentment with the government,” said Priya (name changed), an IT professional from Gurgaon. 

“From something as basic as waterlogged conditions during the monsoon to embezzlement of donations at the Ram Temple at Ayodhya, how much and for how long are we supposed to suffer?” she added. 

Published - July 20, 2026 02:23 am IST

title: Neha, a prominent face of the CJP protests, presses on with hunger strike
author: Nikhil M Babu
content_html:
As the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) started its current protest at Jantar Mantar in New Delhi on June 20, at the peak of Delhi’s punishing summer, the focus was mostly on the founder of the party, Abhijeet Dipke, who had by then become an online sensation.

As the sun went down, the Delhi Police started removing people from Jantar Mantar, and the protest was reduced to a small band of people gathered on and around the main stage. But one lesser-known face, standing right next to Mr. Dipke on his left with an “AISA” badge pinned to her kurta, continued chanting slogans louder than most of the others. She was Ms. Neha.

Published - July 19, 2026 11:24 pm IST

title: CJP protest in Delhi LIVE: Will end fasting today if government takes accountability for failures in education system, says Sonam Wangchuk
author: The Hindu Bureau
content_html:
• July 20, 2026 09:01
    Supporters of CJP gathered for Parliament march

    WhatsApp Image 2026-07-20 at 8.51.17 AM (1).jpeg

  • July 20, 2026 08:18
    Actor Prakash Raj at protest site

    365be91d-052d-4f98-8ed4-0e3ca74dc984.jpg

    Film actor Prakash Raj is seen taking a rest at the protest site in Jantar Mantar, New Delhi, with other protesters.

  • July 20, 2026 07:40
    Delhi Police on high alert ahead of CJP's 'Chalo Sansad' march

    Delhi Police is on high alert ahead of the Monsoon Session of Parliament and the Cockroach Janta Party’s (CJP) proposed “Chalo Sansad” march today, with heavy deployment of personnel visible at every nook and corner around Jantar Mantar, Parliament and other key locations.

    Authorities have also imposed prohibitory orders under Section 163 of the BNSS in the New Delhi district.

    Deputy Commissioner of Police (DCP) Sachin Sharma shared a public advisory on X yesterday, stating that “Delhi Police categorically clarifies that no permission has been sought or granted for any such protest march/procession.” The prohibitory order under Section 163 BNSS (formerly Section 144 CrPC) is currently in effect in the New Delhi district, according to the DCP’s post.

    “Accordingly, protest marches, processions, demonstrations and assemblies of five or more persons are strictly prohibited, except at the designated protest site at Jantar Mantar, with prior permission,” the post read.

    The DCP warned that any person violating these prohibitory orders could face prosecution under Section 223 BNS and other applicable laws.

    -PTI

  • July 20, 2026 07:16
    Truck loaded with stones and a 'wrecked' van shown up near Jantar Mantar

    A “wrecked” van has shown up near Jantar Mantar along with a lorry with stones, Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke said. The CJP plans to start their march to Parliament at 9 a.m. 

    The All India Students’ Association (AISA), three members of whom are on the 23rd day of their hunger strike, said they would receive a delegation urging them to end their hunger strike.

    -Aroon Deep

  • July 20, 2026 07:13
    Will end fasting today if government takes accountability for failures in education system, says Sonam Wangchuk

    Sonam Wangchuk said that he will end his hunger strike on Monday (July 20, 2026) if the government is ready to take accountability for failures in the education system or the MPs are ready to take up the issue in the parliament.

    In a post shared on his X handle, he said his health doesn’t permit him to participate in the Parliament march; the MPs of different parties should meet him at the Safdarjung Hospital to ensure that they will take up the issue of paper leaks in the Parliament.

    He added that his freedom of speech and communication are restricted during his “illegal detention” at the Safdarjung Hospital.

  • July 20, 2026 07:02
    Crowd swells at CJP protest site ahead of Parliament march

    On Sunday (July 19, 2026) afternoon, thousands of people were flowing through both entries to the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protest site at Jantar Mantar in central Delhi. With thousands of people inside the protest area, it was almost impossible to walk without bumping into another person. 

    Read more:

    ​

    Crowd swell at CJP protest site ahead of Parliament march

    CJP's night vigil at Jantar Mantar draws 20,000 supporters ahead of a planned march to Parliament on July 20.

    ​

Published - July 20, 2026 06:56 am IST

title: India advises citizens to postpone travel to Iran amid rising instability
author: PTI
content_html:
India on Sunday (July 19, 2026) advised its nationals to postpone travel to Iran until the security situation improves and urged those already in the country to consider leaving through available commercial flights amid rising instability and conflict.

In a revised travel advisory, the Indian Embassy in Tehran underlined that the "recent days have seen an increase in levels of instability and conflict in Iran", necessitating heightened caution for Indian nationals.

"Indian nationals intending to travel to Iran for any purpose should postpone travel for the time being, until the security environment improves," the mission said.

It advised Indians already in Iran to consider temporarily exiting the country using available flight options.

Those choosing to remain in Iran have been asked to exercise the "highest possible level of caution", the embassy said.

"They should carefully monitor the news to maintain full situational awareness, and should avoid locations seeing higher levels of military activity such as those along the southern coast of the country," it said.

The embassy also urged Indian nationals to comply with instructions issued by local authorities.

It asked all Indians in Iran who have not yet registered with the embassy to do so immediately and to regularly monitor the mission's website and social media handles for further updates.

The advisory comes amid renewed security concerns in Iran following an increase in regional tensions and military activity.

In the last few days, the U.S. has targeted bridges, water desalination plants and electrical facilities in Iran, while Tehran has hit U.S.-allied countries throughout West Asia.

India has, in the past, issued similar advisories during periods of heightened conflict in West Asia and has urged its nationals to remain in contact with the embassy for assistance.

The Indian mission has also shared emergency helpline numbers for citizens requiring assistance.

Published - July 20, 2026 06:39 am IST

title: DMK, TDP should uphold their legacy of ensuring fairness delimitation, says Congress
author: Sobhana K. Nair
content_html:
Invoking the role played by the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) and the Telugu Desam Party (TDP) in previous rounds of delimitation, Congress general secretary (communications) Jairam Ramesh said the two parties should not fritter away their legacy of ensuring fairness in the delimitation process.

Speaking to The Hindu ahead of the Monsoon Session of Parliament, Mr. Ramesh said continuing with the 1971 Census as the basis for the distribution of Lok Sabha seats is crucial to maintaining the federal balance. The Congress, he said, favours extending the freeze for another 25 years, until 2051.

“The DMK played a pivotal role in the Constitutional freeze of 1976. The DMK and the TDP played a crucial role in the Constitutional freeze of 2001. Hopefully both will now continue to play the key role in ensuring there is no unfreezing,” he said.

On April 17, the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, which sought to redistribute Lok Sabha seats on the basis of the 2011 Census and expedite the implementation of women’s reservation, was defeated by a united Opposition. In a House where 528 members were present and voting, 298 voted in favour of the Bill, falling short of the required two-thirds majority.

There is speculation that the government may amend the legislative package comprising the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill and the Delimitation Bill to ensure a proportionate 50% increase in the strength of every State in the Lok Sabha, in an effort to address concerns raised by its allies. The speculation has gained traction after the government’s strength in the Lok Sabha increased after the split in the Trinamool Congress and the Shiv Sena (UBT).

Since the Congress decided to align with the TVK following the defeat of the DMK-led alliance in the Tamil Nadu Assembly election, the DMK has maintained its distance from the Congress, and it remains unclear how it would vote if the Bill returns to Parliament. The TDP, a key BJP ally, too has advocated for retention of the 1971 reference in the legislation.

‘Optical illusion’

The Congress leader said the oral assurance given by Home Minister Amit Shah in the last session regarding a proportionate 50% increase in the Lok Sabha representation of every State was only an “optical illusion” created by the government. The assurance was not reflected in the Bills circulated during the last session. He argued that the issue could not be resolved merely by adding a clause to that effect.

“The talk of a proportionate 50% increase in the strength of each State in the LS is an optical illusion. It is meaningless in the light of Article 81(2)(a) of the Constitution,” Mr. Ramesh said, arguing that the government, through the Bills defeated in the last session, had removed the constitutional protection for maintaining the State-wise proportion of seats in the Lok Sabha.

The legislation sought to amend Article 81(1) to increase the number of Lok Sabha members from 543 to 850, and Article 81(3) to lift the 1971 freeze, while leaving Article 81(2)(a) untouched. This provision maintains that the ratio between a State’s population and its number of Lok Sabha seats should be roughly equal across States. “The Bills circulated by the government in the last session removed the constitutional freeze on the distribution of Lok Sabha seats, which means that it is now a constitutional imperative to reallocate seats in favour of more populous States,” Mr. Ramesh said.

This, he argued, leaves a wide ambit for the Delimitation Commission, which is appointed by the government and whose decisions are protected from judicial challenge under Article 329 of the Constitution.

“The Modi government may commit to a proportionate 50% increase for all States in the Lok Sabha for the 2029 elections. However, after the elections, the commission can very well change the State-wise distribution of Lok Sabha seats, favouring the more populous States in allocation of seats,” he said.

Published - July 19, 2026 08:26 pm IST

  • Copy link
